title: Luscious Turkish Tomatoes Tagine
description: Charred, creamy, nostalgic Turkish dish featuring tomatoes flambéed in coconut milk, served from a traditional clay tajine.
instructions: Warm the tajine clay vessel on a low flame for 5 minutes to reduce cracking risk.
Char the tomatoes by placing them on a hot grill or directly over a gas flame until skin blistered, 3-4 minutes per side, then peel and cut into wedges.
In a skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat and sauté onion and garlic until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add cumin, paprika, coriander, and saffron, and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in charred tomato wedges, coconut milk, sugar, and lemon juice, and bring to a gentle simmer.
Pour the mixture into the warmed tajine, cover, and transfer to the oven for 25 minutes.
While the tagine bakes, heat a small saucepan over low heat, add brandy, and ignite to flambe the tomatoes before draining the flame.
After baking, remove the tajine, sprinkle with fresh parsley, and serve hot with steamed rice or crusty bread.
Garnish with extra lemon wedges if desired.
